label: Small intestine
quick_take: A rare cancer (median ASR around 1) but one of the clearest *rising* trends in the dataset — virtually every high-income registry is going up, and the rise is especially steep in Black populations in the USA.

findings.title: Almost no registry is falling
findings.text: The "biggest decreases" panel is empty for both sexes — unusual in CI5.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Almost no registry is falling

findings.title: US Black populations are climbing fast
findings.text: Louisiana/New Orleans Black men 0.32 → 2.98 (+831 %, inverted-U shape peaking in vol XI); Connecticut Black men +855 %; Louisiana/New Orleans Black women 0.37 → 3.75 (+913 %). Minimum cell-counts are 1–2 cases in early volumes — the magnitudes of percent-change are exaggerated; statewide USA Black (SEER) shows a smaller but consistent rise.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US Black populations are climbing fast

findings.title: New Zealand Māori women have tripled
findings.text: 0.28 (vol II) → 2.08 (vol XII), inverted-U peaking in vol X.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — New Zealand Māori women have tripled

findings.title: Ethnic spread inside the USA is 13×
findings.text: Black populations at the top, Native American and Korean immigrant populations at the bottom.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Ethnic spread inside the USA is 13×

findings.text: Small absolute numbers; any single-registry trend should be read with confidence intervals.

Coding drift between carcinoid and adenocarcinoma over the CI5 era is a known issue — some of the rise may reflect better counting of neuroendocrine tumours (especially in registries with strong pathology). Worth flagging explicitly in any writeup.
The US Black steep rise: obesity/diet, or better histological capture of carcinoids? Stratifying by morphology in SEER-quality registries would help.

The universally male-heavy sex ratio in Europe deserves an explanation — Crohn's-related small-bowel adenocarcinoma? Note most specific registry claims (Poland Cracow 19:1 etc.) are based on single-digit female numerators.
